items.title: Afternoon Briefing: Johnson welcomes latest Bears proposal to keep team in Chicago - Chicago Tribune
items.summary: News |   Afternoon Briefing: Johnson welcomes latest…. # Afternoon Briefing: Johnson welcomes latest Bears proposal to keep team in Chicago. After Illinois lawmakers failed to pass legislation to help the Chicago Bears move to Arlington Heights, Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson welcomed a new proposal as an opening to reconsider Chicago as the site for a new Bears stadium. And remember, for the latest breaking news in Chicago, visit chicagotribune.com/latest-headlines and sign up to get our alerts on all your devices. The current operator of West Suburban Medical Center will remain in place for now, after a Cook County judge rejected a request to place the hospital in receivership today. The Hawks faced a deadline today to sign Jack Pridham, 20, to an entry-level contract or else risk losing his rights if he didn’t commit to a college program and reentered this year’s draft. * ### Mayor Brandon Johnson sees opening in latest stadium proposal to keep Bears in Chicago.
